items.q: What is hydraulic action in a river?
items.correct: The force of moving water itself compressing air into cracks in the river bank or bed, weakening rock until pieces break off — no sediment needed.
items.misconception: Hydraulic action is when rocks in the river smash into each other.
items.random_wrong: Hydraulic action is when the river dissolves rocks chemically.
items.why: Students mix up the four river erosion processes. Hydraulic action = water force; abrasion = water using rocks to scrape; attrition = rocks hitting rocks; solution (corrosion) = chemical dissolving.

items.q: Why has the Richter scale been largely replaced by the moment magnitude scale?
items.correct: Richter saturates above magnitude ~7 and was calibrated for local Californian quakes; moment magnitude (Mw) measures the total energy released and works for all sizes globally.
items.misconception: The Richter scale is still the standard scale used by all seismologists.
items.random_wrong: Moment magnitude measures damage while Richter measures shaking.
items.why: Media still says 'Richter' but seismologists now report Mw. Both are logarithmic — a magnitude 7 releases ~32x more energy than a 6 — but Mw is the modern standard for large quakes.

items.q: Why do some rivers form deltas and others form estuaries?
items.correct: Deltas form where rivers deposit sediment faster than the sea can remove it (calm seas, heavy load); estuaries form where tidal energy removes sediment (strong tides).
items.misconception: Deltas form on big rivers and estuaries on small ones.
items.random_wrong: Deltas form in cold climates and estuaries in warm climates.
items.why: Size doesn't determine the form. The balance of sediment supply vs marine energy does — the Nile (delta) and Thames (estuary) are similar size but in different sea conditions.

items.q: Which two plates are colliding to build the Himalayas?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Indian and Eurasian. “Pacific and North American” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
The answer examiners want is: Glacial lake outburst flood. “Glacier loss observation framework” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Approximately how many people downstream depend on Himalayan-fed rivers (in billions)?
items.why: The real figure is 2. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
